etymology.description: 你(你) (nǐ, 'you') + 好(好) (hǎo, 'good') creates a friendly greeting similar to 'hello' in English.
etymology.interpretation: The phrase 你好 (nǐ hǎo) directly translates to 'you good', which when used together forms a standard greeting. The combination reflects a polite inquiry into someone's well-being, acknowledging the other person with a positive and open expression, akin to saying 'hello' in English.

examples.chinese: 你好(你好)，我很高兴认识你(我很高興認識你)。
examples.pinyin: Nǐ hǎo, wǒ hěn gāoxìng rènshi nǐ.
examples.english: Hello, I'm very happy to meet you.

examples.chinese: 每天早上见面时(每天早上見面時)，我们都会说“你好”(我們都會說“你好”)。
examples.pinyin: Měitiān zǎoshang jiànmiàn shí, wǒmen dōu huì shuō “nǐ hǎo”.
examples.english: Every morning when we meet, we all say 'hello'.

examples.chinese: 她总是用愉快的语气说“你好”(她總是用愉快的語氣說“你好”)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zǒngshì yòng yúkuài de yǔqì shuō “nǐ hǎo”.
examples.english: She always says 'hello' with a cheerful tone.
